chapter: Chapter: Weavers, Iron Smelters and Factory Owners

questions.question: Explain why Indian textiles were in great demand in international markets during the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries.
questions.answer: Indian textiles were highly valued because of their fine quality, durability and beautiful designs. European traders bought large quantities of Indian cotton and silk cloth for sale in world markets.

questions.question: How did the East India Company gain control over the Indian textile trade?
questions.answer: The East India Company used political power and its agents called gomasthas to control weavers and ensure a steady supply of cloth for export.

questions.question: Describe the role of gomasthas in controlling Indian weavers.
questions.answer: Gomasthas supervised weavers, provided advances and forced them to supply cloth only to the Company at fixed prices.

questions.question: Why did many weavers accept advances from the East India Company?
questions.answer: Weavers accepted advances because they needed money to buy raw materials, but this forced them to sell cloth only to the Company.

questions.question: Explain how the Industrial Revolution affected Indian textile producers.
questions.answer: The Industrial Revolution introduced machines that produced cloth cheaply and quickly, reducing demand for Indian handwoven textiles.

questions.question: Why were Indian weavers unable to compete with British factory-made textiles?
questions.answer: British factory-made textiles were cheaper and produced in large quantities using machines, making it difficult for handloom weavers to compete.

questions.question: Explain how the arrival of British manufactured goods affected Indian handloom industries.
questions.answer: British manufactured goods flooded Indian markets and reduced demand for traditional handwoven cloth.

questions.question: What changes occurred in textile production after the introduction of factories?
questions.answer: Textile production shifted from handloom weaving to machine-based production in factories.

questions.question: Why did factory industries develop in India in the late nineteenth century?
questions.answer: Factories developed due to the demand for textiles, availability of raw materials and the influence of industrial technology.

questions.question: Describe the role of Indian entrepreneurs in the development of textile mills.
questions.answer: Indian entrepreneurs invested money, established textile mills and helped develop modern industries.

questions.question: Explain the importance of cotton in the textile industry.
questions.answer: Cotton was the main raw material used in textile production and played a major role in the growth of textile industries.

questions.question: Why did traditional iron smelting decline during British rule?
questions.answer: Traditional iron smelting declined due to the import of cheap British iron and restrictions on forest resources.

questions.question: Explain why forests were important for iron smelters.
questions.answer: Iron smelters needed wood from forests to produce charcoal, which was used as fuel in iron smelting.

questions.question: How did British forest policies affect iron smelters?
questions.answer: British forest policies restricted access to forests, making it difficult for iron smelters to obtain wood for fuel.

questions.question: Why did many iron smelters lose their traditional occupation?
questions.answer: They lost their occupation due to forest restrictions and competition from imported iron.

questions.question: Explain how railways supported the growth of industries in India.
questions.answer: Railways helped transport raw materials to factories and finished goods to markets.

questions.question: Why did the demand for factory-made cloth increase?
questions.answer: Factory-made cloth was cheaper and produced in large quantities, making it more available to consumers.

questions.question: Explain how industrialization changed traditional production systems.
questions.answer: Industrialization replaced small-scale manual production with machine-based factory production.

questions.question: Why did many traditional industries decline during colonial rule?
questions.answer: Traditional industries declined because they could not compete with cheaper machine-made products.

questions.question: What role did factory owners play in the development of modern industries?
questions.answer: Factory owners invested capital, organized production and introduced new technologies.

questions.question: Explain the importance of machines in the textile industry.
questions.answer: Machines increased the speed and quantity of production, making textile manufacturing more efficient.

questions.question: How did industrialization affect workers and artisans?
questions.answer: Many artisans lost their traditional occupations and were forced to work as laborers in factories.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between factories and modern industry.
questions.answer: Factories introduced machine-based production, which became the foundation of modern industry.

questions.question: Why was industrialization considered an important economic change?
questions.answer: Industrialization increased production, created new industries and changed traditional economic systems.

questions.question: What was the long-term impact of industrialization on Indian society?
questions.answer: Industrialization led to the growth of factories, decline of traditional crafts and the rise of modern industries.
